chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يُمْتَحَنُ بِهِ مَنْ يُصَابُ فِي سَمْعِهِ أَوْ بَصَرِهِ أَوْ غَيْرِ ذَلِكَ مِنْ جَوَارِحِهِ وَ الْقِيَاسِ فِي ذَلِكَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 31 – What he is tested with, the one who is hit regards to his hearing, or his sight, or other than that from his limbs, and the quantification regarding that

text: هُ قَالَ فِي رَجُلٍ ضَرَبَ رَجُلًا فِي أُذُنِهِ بِعَظْمٍ فَادَّعَى أَنَّهُ لَا يَسْمَعُ قَالَ يُتَرَصَّدُ وَ يُسْتَغْفَلُ وَ يُنْتَظَرُ بِهِ سَنَةً فَإِنْ سَمِعَ أَوْ شَهِدَ عَلَيْهِ رَجُلَانِ أَنَّهُ يَسْمَعُ وَ إِلَّا حَلَّفَهُ وَ أَعْطَاهُ الدِّيَةَ
قِيلَ يَا أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ فَإِنْ عُثِرَ عَلَيْهِ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ أَنَّهُ يَسْمَعُ قَالَ إِنْ كَانَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ رَدَّ عَلَيْهِ سَمْعَهُ لَمْ أَرَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْئاً .

en.hubeali: From him, from his father, and Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, both together from Ibn Mahboub, from Abu Ayoub, from Suleyman Bin Khalid,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said with regards to a man who struck a man in his ear with a bone, so he claimed that he cannot hear. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘He would be placed under surveillance and would be awaited with for a year. So if he hears, or two men testify against him that he can hear, or else he would be made to swear an oath, and he would be given the wergild’.

It was said, ‘O Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup>! So if it was discovered afterwards that he can hear?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic Returns his hearing, I<sup>asws</sup> do not see anything upon him’.
